content:
# Thin-Section Petrography

**Summary**: The study of rock thin sections under a polarizing microscope to determine mineralogy, texture, and pore characteristics.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995]] used thin-section-petrography to derive grain size distribution (GSD) and pore size distribution (PSD).

- [[1996-matter|Matter, 1996]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize the mineralogical composition and texture of 50 phosphorite samples.

- [[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000]] used thin-section-petrography to identify lithology and estimate porosity.

- [[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize the petrographic features of Jauf sandstone samples.

- [[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992]] used thin-section-petrography to analyze the microfacies and stratigraphy of the Jabal Hafeet succession.

- [[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize detrital and authigenic components in the Unayzah Formation.

- [[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992]] applied thin-section-petrography to over 30 core samples for petrographic description.

- [[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988]] applied thin-section petrography to characterize the lithofacies of the core samples.

- [[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006]] used thin-section-petrography to examine foraminiferal biocomponents in the Dam Formation.

- [[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010]] used thin-section-petrography to determine sedimentary facies and link them to petrophysical rock types.

- [[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize carbonate minerals and textures in the Lower Fadhili reservoir.

- [[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize carbonate facies in the Arab-C reservoir.

- [[2012-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2012]] used petrography to determine the mineral composition of samples from Ruman N1 and N2 wells.

- [[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013]] applied thin-section petrography to define lithofacies and characterize diagenetic processes like micritization and dolomitization.

- [[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014]] applied thin-section petrography to characterize the grain types (QFL) and diagenetic features of Sarah Formation sandstones.

- [[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize the textural variability and its correlation with porosity and permeability.

- [[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize lithofacies and identify various porosity types in the Khartam Member.

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize microfacies and biocomponents in 120 samples.

- [[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize the petrographic properties of the Siq, Quweira, and Saq Sandstones.

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize lithofacies and identify minerals in the Qusaiba Shale.

- [[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015]] applied thin-section petrography to characterize microfacies and diagenetic features in the Musayr Formation and Wadi Waqb Member.

- [[2015-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2015]] used thin-section petrography to identify lithofacies using the Dunham classification.

- [[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015]] applied thin-section petrography to characterize microfacies and mineralogy.

- [[2015-ali|Ali, 2015]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize carbonate-siliciclastic components in the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] used thin-section petrography to define lithofacies and quantify minerals.

- [[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]] used thin-section petrography to characterize the lithofacies of the Dam Formation.

- [[2016-babker|Babker, 2016]] used thin-section petrography to analyze the lithology of the Sarah Formation.

- [[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016]] applied thin-section-petrography to characterize the mineralogy and texture of 15 lithofacies.

- [[2016-malik|Malik, 2016]] used thin-section petrography to identify microfacies and biofacies in the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016]] used thin-section petrography on 92 samples to describe mineralogical composition and texture.

- [[2016-salih|Salih, 2016]] performed detailed thin section petrography to identify texture, mineralogy, grain types, bioclasts content, cement types, granulometry, and pore-type distribution.

- [[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize microfacies types I through X.

- [[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016]] used thin-section petrography to differentiate carbonate minerals and visualize pore systems.

- [[2016-septriandi|Septriandi, 2016]] used thin-section-petrography to identify larger foraminiferal fragments like alveolinids and lepidocyclinids.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] used thin sections to define different lithofed facies in the study area.

- [[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017]] used thin sections for detailed core description and lithofacies identification.

- [[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017]] used thin-section petrography to analyze the mineralogy and texture of samples.

- [[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017]] used thin-section petrography to analyze the lithofacies and textures of the Sarah Formation.

- [[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017]] used thin-section petrography to characterize microfacies and diagenetic features.

- [[2017-adam|Adam, 2017]] applied thin-section petrography to characterize lithofacies and diagenetic features in the Upper Khartam Member.

- [[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize lithofacies and paleoenvironments in the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2018-butt|Butt, 2018]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize the petrology and diagenesis of the Nutaysh member.

- [[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018]] used thin-section petrography to define lithofacies in the Qusaiba Formation.

- [[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018]] used thin-section petrography to characterize microfacies and determine mineral proportions.

- [[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020]] used thin-section petrography to identify diagenetic fabrics like micritization and dissolution.

- [[2020-islam|Islam, 2020]] used thin-section-petrography to classify sandstones as sub-arkose to quartz arenite.

- [[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize lithofacies and porosity types in Jurassic carbonates.

- [[2021-ahmed|Ahmed, 2021]] performed thin-section petrography using an Olympus BX53 microscope to characterize pore types and diagenetic features.

- [[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize lithofacies and mineralogy in the Sarah formation.

- [[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize microfacies and identify diagenetic minerals in the Monte Sacro Sequence.

- [[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022]] used thin-section-petrography to identify benthic foraminifera in the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2022-khan|Khan, 2022]] used thin-section petrography to identify lithofacies and mineral textures.

- [[2022-syahputra|Syahputra, 2022]] applied petrography to characterize rock textures (wackestone/packstone) and burrow attributes in the Ulayyah Member.

- [[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022]] applied thin-section petrography to identify pore types and diagenetic features.

- [[2022-salih|Salih, 2022]] applied thin-section petrography to characterize lithofacies and pore systems in the studied carbonate formations.

- [[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize the lithofacies of the Hanifa Formation.

- [[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022]] used thin-section petrography to analyze the mineralogy of samples from proximal to distal sites.

- [[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022]] applied thin-section analysis to determine the age and lithology of carbonate samples.

- [[2022-babker|Babker, 2022]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize lithofacies at the macro-scale.

- [[2023-butt|Butt, 2023]] used thin-section petrography to characterize lithofacies and mineralogy.

- [[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023]] used thin-section-petrography to identify petrographic characteristics and qualitative modal mineralogy.

- [[2023-rafiq|Rafiq, 2023]] used thin-section-petrography to investigate mineralogical and textural features of the rock samples.

- [[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]] used thin-section-petrography to identify five microfacies in the Valanginian carbonates.

- [[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024]] applied thin-section petrography to identify mineralogical compositions, textures, and micro-fractures in 36 samples.

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize the lithology and pore systems of the Dhaiqa Formation.

- [[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024]] applied thin-section petrography to characterize the mineralogy and texture of the Al-Ghurayyah pluton.

- [[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024]] applied thin-section petrography to characterize rock constituents and laminae in the Dam Formation.

- [[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024]] used thin-section-petrography to distinguish between host rock matrix (HRM) and burrow fill (BF).

- [[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024]] used thin-section analysis to establish biozones and sequence stratigraphy in the Dhruma Formation.

- [[2025-refaat|Refaat, 2025]] applied thin-section-petrography to identify burrowing styles and diagenetic modifications.

- [[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize the lithology and microfacies of the Gudair Formation.

- [[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992]] applied thin-section petrography to 193 samples from the Uthmaniyah area to identify lithofacies.

- [[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999]] used thin-section-petrography to characterize the lithofacies and mineralogy of the Wajid Sandstone.

- [[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994]] used thin-section-petrography on one lithified aragonitic layer to examine mineralogy and texture.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Petrographic point-counting provided a viable shortcut for obtaining rapid estimates of hydrogeologic inputs like porosity and permeability. ([[1995-magaji|Magaji, 1995, p. 17]])

- Identified carbonate-apatite as the primary mineral with calcite and dolomite as dominant gangue minerals. ([[1996-matter|Matter, 1996, p. 58]])

- Thin sections were used to identify rock types and estimate porosities. ([[2000-mohammad|Mohammad, 2000, p. 17]])

- Used to identify porosity through 200-point counts and visual charts for percentage calculation. ([[2001-al-ramadan|Al-Ramadan, 2001, p. 29]])

- Identified five distinctive microfacies used to interpret age and paleoenvironmental conditions. ([[1992-el-sahn|El-Sahn, 1992, p. 13]])

- Identified calcite, clay, and quartz overgrowths as major authigenic cements. ([[2002-siddiqui|Siddiqui, 2002, p. 23]])

- Thin sections were used to identify lithofacies and provide detailed descriptions of the carbonate rocks in the Shu'aiba and Yamama formations. ([[1992-al-sulami|Al-Sulami, 1992, p. 27]])

- Petrographic analysis identified five distinct non-dolomitic lithofacies including oolitic sand and skeletal grainstone. ([[1988-darwish|Darwish, 1988, p. 12]])

- Identified 51 species of foraminifera within the Dam Formation carbonate rocks. ([[2006-al-enezi|Al-Enezi, 2006, p. 13]])

- Thin sections were used to define geological facies which are then linked to petrophysical rock property groups. ([[2010-alroudhan|AlRoudhan, 2010, p. 46]])

- Identified five distinct facies based on petrographic and sedimentological characteristics. ([[2010-al-mojel|Al-Mojel, 2010, p. 13]])

- Identified several distinct carbonate types including mollusk-rich ooid grainstones and thrombolites. ([[2011-busubait|Busubait, 2011, p. 31]])

- Identified mineral assemblage as granodioritic gneiss consisting of quartz, hornblende, K-feldspar, plagioclase, and biotite. ([[2012-abdelgader|Abdelgader, 2012, p. 36]])

- Identified several diagenetic stages including early dolomitization, dissolution, and cementation. ([[2013-abdalla|Abdalla, 2013, p. 74]])

- Identified monocrystalline quartz as the dominant component in several samples, with varying degrees of iron cementation. ([[2014-waseem|Waseem, 2014, p. 13]])

- Showed good correlation between the textural variability and porosity and permeability measurements. ([[2014-el-deek|El-Deek, 2014, p. 16]])

- Identified three distinct lithofacies: Well-Sorted Oolitic Grainstone, Cross-bedded Oolitic Skeletal Grainstone, and Graded Mudstone to Packstone. ([[2014-mohammed|Mohammed, 2014, p. 41]])

- Thin sections revealed that the dominant grain types were very fine to fine ooids, detrital peloids, and calcisiltites. ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014, p. 93]])

- Identified various sedimentary structures and minerals in the clastic units through detailed microscopic analysis. ([[2014-mohammedfaiz|Mohammedfaiz, 2014, p. 168]])

- Identified three distinct mudstone facies based on petrographic characteristics. ([[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015, p. 19]])

- Identified oolitic grainstone with suture contacts and foraminiferal packstone characterized by miliolid forams. ([[2015-koeshidayatullah|Koeshidayatullah, 2015, p. 40]])

- Identified several lithofacies including dolomitic mudstone, stromatoporoid packstone, and various grainstones. ([[2015-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2015, p. 10]])

- Used in conjunction with alizarin red staining to differentiate carbonate minerals. ([[2015-elzain|Elzain, 2015, p. 56]])

- Identified 17 distinct lithofacies including oolitic grainstone, stromatolites, and mud-cracked shales. ([[2015-ali|Ali, 2015, p. 14]])

- Identified 9 distinct lithofacies (LF1-LF9) based on grain size, sorting, and cementation. ([[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016, p. 13]])

- Not stated in source ([[2016-hassan-2|Hassan, 2016]])

- Used to investigate lithological heterogeneity from macro to micro scale. ([[2016-babker|Babker, 2016, p. 12]])

- Thin section analysis was used to distinguish between various carbonate and clastic facies. ([[2016-bashri|Bashri, 2016, p. 14]])

- Thin section analysis revealed specific textures such as micritization in Fp-g facies and oolitic structures in Bog facies. ([[2016-malik|Malik, 2016, p. 12]])

- Identified bedding-parallel stylolites as common in limestone and dolomitic limestone lithologies. ([[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016, p. 19]])

- Thin-section petrography was used to differentiate calcite from dolomite using Alizarin Red S stain. ([[2016-salih|Salih, 2016, p. 46]])

- Petrography identified 10 distinct microfabric types in the carbonate samples. ([[2016-radwan|Radwan, 2016, p. 36]])

- Alizarin Red staining was used in thin sections to distinguish between different carbonate minerals. ([[2016-makkawi|Makkawi, 2016, p. 39]])

- Identified large foraminiferal fragments (alveolinids, lepidocyclinids) in sample 10. ([[2016-septriandi|Septriandi, 2016, p. 65]])

- Used alongside SEM and XRD for petrographic characterization. ([[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017, p. 19]])

- Used to characterize the spatial distribution of Tuwaiq Mountain lithofacies at multiple scales. ([[2017-mohammed|Mohammed, 2017, p. 23]])

- Used to characterize 17 distinct lithofacies. ([[2017-mohammed-2|Mohammed, 2017, p. 15]])

- Identified four distinct lithofacies (FA1-FA4) based on textural and mineralogical characteristics. ([[2017-alqubalee|Alqubalee, 2017, p. 9]])

- Identified various carbonate textures including rudstone, wackestone, and grainstone. ([[2017-elyamani|ElYamani, 2017, p. 67]])

- Lithofacies were identified as oolitic grainstone, recrystallized limestone, and marlstone through microscopic observation. ([[2017-adam|Adam, 2017, p. 15]])

- Identified 9 distinct lithofacies (LFT1-LFT9) based on petrographic analysis of the D5 and D6 members. ([[2017-yousif|Yousif, 2017, p. 86]])

- Identified various cement types including calcite, pyrite, and hematite, as well as secondary porosity from grain dissolution. ([[2018-butt|Butt, 2018, p. 127]])

- Identified several mudstone and siltstone facies (Mdg, Mlg, Mzr) based on mineralogy and texture. ([[2018-perdana|Perdana, 2018, p. 36]])

- Identified 8 distinct microfacies based on petrographic analysis. ([[2018-ismanto|Ismanto, 2018, p. 17]])

- Identified 7 distinct diagenetic categories including micrite envelopes and neomorphic fabrics. ([[2020-al-zoukani|Al-Zoukani, 2020, p. 75]])

- Sandstone was classified into the sub-arkose to quartz arenite range based on QFL counts. ([[2020-islam|Islam, 2020, p. 71]])

- Identified mudstones, wackestones, packstones, and grainstones as common lithofacies. ([[2021-al-nuwais|Al-Nuwais, 2021, p. 13]])

- Identified moldic pores, microfractures, intergranular porosity, and burrow-related porosity as primary macro-porosity creators. ([[2021-ahmed|Ahmed, 2021, p. 67]])

- Used to identify mineralogical composition, grain size characteristics, sorting, and diagenetic features like cementation. ([[2021-alqarni|Alqarni, 2021, p. 37]])

- Identified multiple microfacies (MF1-MF10) including oolitic grainstones and peloidal packstones. ([[2021-alabyadh|Alabyadh, 2021, p. 83]])

- Identification was possible for 89.93% of specimens, indicating good preservation. ([[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022, p. 38]])

- Identified various lithofacies such as oolitic grainstone, quartz sandstone, and bioclastic limestone. ([[2022-khan|Khan, 2022, p. 64]])

- Petrography identified two distinct burrowing styles: bio-retexturing and pipe framework. ([[2022-syahputra|Syahputra, 2022, p. 41]])

- Identified 5 pore types including intercrystal non-fabric preserved (BC-nfp) and intraparticle (WP). ([[2022-stiawan|Stiawan, 2022, p. 18]])

- Identified various pore types including oomoldic, interparticle, vug, and micro-intercrystalline pores. ([[2022-salih|Salih, 2022, p. 30]])

- Identified distinct lithofacies such as F1 (argillaceous carbonate mudstone) and F3 (sponge spiculitic wackestone). ([[2022-bashri|Bashri, 2022, p. 35]])

- Identified distinct mineral grains (plagioclase, pyroxene) across different environments. ([[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022, p. 58]])

- Thin-section analysis revealed Borelis melo, confirming a middle Miocene age for the carbonates. ([[2022-al-sadah|Al-Sadah, 2022, p. 14]])

- Used for characterizing LFA-1, LFA-2, LFA-3, LFA-4, LFA-5, and LFA-6. ([[2022-babker|Babker, 2022, p. 11]])

- Petrographic point-count data were used to determine reservoir properties like porosity. ([[2023-butt|Butt, 2023, p. 124]])

- Identification of petrographic characteristics and qualitative modal mineralogy (mode) of polished thin sections was acquired by using a Zeiss microscope. ([[2023-iskandar|Iskandar, 2023, p. 48]])

- Identified quartz veins, pockets, orthoclase (cross-hatched twinning), and chlorite. ([[2023-rafiq|Rafiq, 2023, p. 72]])

- Identified five microfacies representing a carbonate ramp setting from inner to outer ramp. ([[2024-allam|Allam, 2024, p. 11]])

- Thin sections were used to distinguish between open, filled, and occluded fractures across three lithostratigraphic units. ([[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024, p. 79]])

- 35-micron thick, unpolished thin sections were used to identify pores and determine sedimentary facies. ([[2024-malik|Malik, 2024, p. 29]])

- Identified four components: Arfvedsonite granite, amphibole-free porphyritic coarse/medium-grained granite, and microgranite. ([[2024-sulistyohariyanto|Sulistyohariyanto, 2024, p. 15]])

- Identified distinct lamination with alternating micrite-rich (tan) and grain-rich (blue) laminae in CF forms. ([[2024-muharrag|Muharrag, 2024, p. 22]])

- Identified distinct boundaries between muddy, well-cemented HRM and coarse-grained, porous BF in BN styles. ([[2024-allehyani|allehyani, 2024, p. 63]])

- Used to define foraminiferal assemblages in subsurface sections before this study's outcrop focus. ([[2024-jalloh|Jalloh, 2024, p. 19]])

- Thin-sections revealed two distinct burrowing styles: pipe framework (mud-poor) and bio-retexturing. ([[2025-refaat|Refaat, 2025, p. 28]])

- Identified 10 distinct microfacies types in the Umm Gudair#1 well. ([[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989, p. 16]])

- Analysis identified six distinct units worthy of sub-member rank within the Arab-D Limestone Member. ([[1992-abdulghani|Abdulghani, 1992, p. 18]])

- Identified quartz, feldspar, and lithic grains (QFL) as primary components in both Red and Grey units. ([[1999-babalola|Babalola, 1999, p. 43]])

- Identified the presence of acicular aragonitic cement inside shells and other minerals in the sedimentary matrix. ([[1994-al-guwaizani|Al-Guwaizani, 1994, p. 22]])
